commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
In him, to instruct and enable him both to rout the enemy, and to govern the people with prudence. (Haydock)
Chaldean, "the spirit of prophecy. "The oracle excited him to attack Chusan. (Josephus, v. 3.) He was entrusted with an extraordinary authority, in a wonderful manner, and God gave him all those virtues which were requisite for his exalted station. (Calmet)
Him. Hebrew, "his hand was strong upon Chusan Rasathaim. "He gained a complete victory over him, (Haydock) the particulars of which are not mentioned, though they must have been very interesting and extraordinary, as the power of Chusan was so extensive. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
